summary: Wraps Stein's binary GCD with a tuple-return `gcd_lcm` computing `(gcd(x,y), x * (y / gcd))` on u64; the master `gcd_lcm_spec` closed form is proved under the precondition `x.toNat * y.toNat < 2^64` by composing the proven `gcd_spec` (black-box reuse of the full Stein machinery — trailing-zeros loop, gcd_stein_loop subtract-and-strip, gcd-shift-combine outer wrapper, or-divisibility) with downstream division + multiplication via the gcd-lcm product identity `g * l = x * y` (`Nat.mul_div_assoc` with `Nat.gcd_dvd_right`), and mul-no-overflow on the derived value `x * (y/g)` is discharged from the precondition by bounding `(y/g) ≤ y ⇒ x*(y/g) ≤ x*y`; 4-way zero-input boundary dispatch (`(0,0)`, `(x,0)`, `(0,y)`, both-nonzero) with two custom `mul_zero_{left,right}_pure` helpers proving `UInt64.mulOverflow` is false when one operand is zero, and 9 contract theorems (two doc-test unit pins, three zero-boundary clauses, totality, `g ∣ x`, `g ∣ y`, gcd-zero-iff-both-inputs-zero, greatest-common-divisor maximality via `Nat.dvd_gcd`, product identity `g.toNat * l.toNat = x.toNat * y.toNat`) all project from the single master spec.
